title: APEX WAVE-EXIT-INVARIANTS.json
description: Wave-exit invariants metadata artifact (one per wave per round). Lists invariants the wave-executor TEMPORARILY relaxed at wave close, plus the R-item slated to re-assert each. Future audits read this artifact to distinguish 'expected scope-bounded relaxation' from 'silent regression'. Spec anchors: 'Honest scope over marketing scope.' + 'Proof-of-process beats proof-of-promise.' (apex-spec.md, principles section).

round.description: Self-heal round number (R<N>) the wave belongs to.

wave_number.description: Wave number within the round (1-indexed, matches WAVES-R<N>.md).

wave_status.description: Terminal status of the wave at exit. Mirrors WAVE-<X>-RESULT.md status field.

exit_timestamp.description: ISO-8601 timestamp captured by the wave-executor at wave close.

relaxed_invariants.description: Each element documents one invariant the wave temporarily relaxed and the future R-item slated to re-assert it. Empty array means the wave exited with all baseline invariants intact.

invariant_id.description: Stable identifier for the relaxed invariant (e.g., 'TOTALS_INVARIANT', 'SYNC_DELIVERY_CONTRACT', 'COVERAGE_FLOOR'). Free-form; chosen for grep-ability.

relaxation_reason.description: One-sentence rationale: why the wave could not enforce the invariant at exit. Must be falsifiable (a future auditor can verify the claim).

reassertion_r_id.description: R-item slated to re-assert the invariant in a later wave/round (e.g., 'R9-002').

reassertion_wave.description: Wave number in which the reassertion R-item is scheduled.

evidence_anchor.description: Optional pointer to evidence (path:anchor or commit hash) showing the relaxation is bounded and recoverable.
